observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) require the General Services Office and Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile
their records of PPE accounts;
b) ensure that the RPCPPE includes all the necessary information and shall include
only serviceable PPE which are physically counted; and
c) enjoin the members of the Inventory Committee to speed up the preparation and
actual conduct of physical inventory activities for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE
in accordance with COA Circular No. 202-006 to lessen, if not totally eliminate,
the discrepancy between actually existing and recorded PPEs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) facilitate the completion of the documents needed to file the request for write-off
to the COA of the receivable due from the deceased former Municipal Mayor
amounting to P42,650.55 which had been dormant for 36 years; and
b) follow-up the status of the case filed at Branch 16 Cavite City Trial Court against
the two Accountable Officers and to include the amounts due them in the request
for write-off only if the cases against them are already dismissed as evidenced by
certification or certified copies of finality of decision from the said courts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to remit
all taxes withheld within the prescribed period to avoid any liability equivalent to the
surcharges that may be imposed by the BIR against the latter and other responsible
officials in their personal capacity.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and
other concerned officials to review and verify records pertaining to outstanding
balances of Due to GSIS from prior years totaling P639,487.72. Thereafter, remit
immediately all insurance premiums and loan payments withheld due to the GSIS as
of December 31, 2022 to avoid the incurrence of penalties and surcharges due to late
remittances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) require the concerned accountable officer to immediately liquidate the
outstanding cash advance amounting to P486,585.07, to prevent the withholding
or suspension of her salaries for non-compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002;
and
b) direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advances
unless complete liquidation papers of the previous cash advance are submitted.
Thereafter, ensure strict compliance with the above regulations on the grant,
utilization and liquidation of cash advances, particularly the period within which to
liquidate the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) enjoin the Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal Accountant and other
concerned officials to provide valid explanation or justification on the
circumstances behind the expenditures beyond the appropriations amounting to
P9,744,714.08 for evaluation of the Audit Team, otherwise will be disallowed in
audit;
b) direct the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly ensure that the actual expenditures
do not exceed the budget for each class/fund and that they are utilized solely for
the purpose for which they were created/intended; and
c) require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to closely
monitor the balances of appropriations by the use of RAAOs and to advise the
concerned officials and employees to stop the incurrence of obligations when
appropriations are no longer available for the purpose.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) enjoin the Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned
officials to submit to the Audit Team the basis used in the computation of the
target collectible business income for CY 2022 and valid explanation on the three-
year continuous failure of the Municipality to realize said target; and
b) direct the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly set a more realistic budget based on
previous year’s actual performance in terms of revenues and expenses in
accordance with the existing guidelines on budget preparation.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
a) direct the Municipal Budget Officer, Accountant and Treasurer submit a specific,
measurable, attainable, relevant and time-bound action plan that will outline their
recommended fiscal policies and strategies to address the problem of cash
overdraft that the Municipality had been experiencing over the years; and
b) instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Treasurer to refrain from using the
Special Education and Trust Funds for purposes other than those for which these
funds are intended, otherwise necessary charges should be filed against them.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC and/or other concerned
officials and employees to submit to the COA Audit Team the lacking documents
and/or a written satisfactory justification on the deficiencies noted in the
procurement, otherwise the transactions will be suspended or disallowed in audit.
Henceforth, it is recommended that the BAC subject all procurement of goods to
public bidding except when the conditions and circumstances warrant the use of
Alternative Methods of Procurement as provided under the pertinent provisions of RA
No. 9184 and its revised IRR.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) coordinate with the concerned officials and submit immediately to the Audit Team
the documents or reports detailing the composition of items purchased and paid
with Check Nos. 1122999 and 1123141 dated February 28, 2022 and March 24,
2022, respectively, indicating the quantity, description of the articles, unit and
total value otherwise the amount of P5,080,000.00 will be suspended in audit; and
b) ensure that succeeding payments for procurements are supported with complete
documentary requirements as prescribed under the provisions RA No. 9184 and
COA Circular No. 2012-001 to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) direct the Municipal Accountant to submit all the required documents as
enumerated in Item 9.1.3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to the Audit Team for
further review and evaluation;
b) instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to submit a valid explanation on why there
was a significant increase in the hauling and disposal of garbage for further
review and evaluation of the Audit Team; and
c) require the Municipal Budget Officer, Accountant and Treasurer to exercise
prudence and control in the disposition of government funds by prioritizing,
scrutinizing, and evaluating expenses to be incurred.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LDRRMO and other
concerned officials to ensure that distributions of various welfare goods are
supported by original copy of the list of recipients with their names, addresses,
contact numbers, and signatures, and the description and quantity of the items
they had received to facilitate validation by the COA Audit Team and other
stakeholders, otherwise relevant disbursements may be suspended or disallowed
in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) Secretariat to post the procurement opportunity for all modes of procurement
in the PhilGEPS and at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the
premises of the Municipality within three (3) calendar days and attached a copy of the
posted advertisement to the DV for Audit Team’s validation, otherwise pertinent
disbursements will be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and other
concerned officials to prepare and submit the necessary requirements to enable the
Municipal to include in its automated payroll system the payment of the wages of its
Job Order workers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory and Disposal
Committee to exert all possible means to locate the missing four units of
unserviceable motor vehicles and thereafter, facilitate the immediate disposal of the
same along with the other unserviceable properties of the Municipality, submit the
duly accomplished IIRUP supported with all pertinent disposal documents to the
Municipal Accountant for review and dropping from the books. Should efforts to
locate the missing vehicles proved futile, file a request for relief from property
accountability to the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
immediately process the increase of amount of bond of the DO who handles cash
Advances for payroll, at least until full implementation of the automated payroll
system in the Municipality, and refrain from granting cash advances to accountable
officers beyond the maximum accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator and other concerned officials to thoroughly plan the start
and target completion dates and timetable of the procurement of all development
projects and integrate them in the AIP and APP to facilitate timely completion of the
projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GFPS, through the GAD
Focal Person, to ensure that all PPAs included in the GPB have well-defined gender
issues or GAD mandate to be addressed and to completely fill out the GAD
Accomplishment Report in accordance with Item C.8.1 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and
NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: b. require the General Services Office to update the Property Cards based on the results of the physical inventory

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO and Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain SLC and SC, respectively, for each type of supplies for the proper monitoring of the receipts, issuances, and balances of the assets by said officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer, Accountant and Treasurer submit a specific, measurable, attainable, relevant and time-bound action plan that will outline their recommended fiscal policies and strategies to address the problem of cash overdraft that the Municipality had been experiencing over the years; and instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Treasurer to refrain from using the Special Education and Trust Funds for purposes other than those for which these funds are intended.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer, and the Municipal Accountant commented that they will continue to exert efforts to further reduce the cash overdraft and promised to submit to the Municipal Mayor a specific, measurable, attainable, relevant and time-bound action plan that will outline their recommended fiscal policies and strategies to address the problem of cash overdraft that the Municipality had been experiencing over the years. Likewise, they will also refrain from using the Special Education and Trust Funds for purposes other than those for which these funds are intended.
observations.reason: Due to Cash overdraft, the Municipality still resorted to borrowing from SEF and TF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to release to the Provincial and Barangay Treasurers the provincial and barangay shares from the RPT collections of the Municipality on time so that the concerned local government units could utilize the funds for their projects.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and the Cashier deposited all collections intact and promptly and refrain from directly utilizing them in the operation of the Municipality later in CY 2022.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to provide the Municipal Treasurer with the List of Suppliers/Contractors who have transacted with the Municipality in the preceding year together with the total payments made to them as primary reference in the assessment of the business taxes that will be collected from them.

The Municipal Accountant provided the Municipal Treasurer with the List of Suppliers/Contractors who have transacted with the Municipality in the preceding year together with the total payments made to them as primary reference in the assessment of the business taxes that will be collected from them.

observations.managementAction: The Management maximized the utilization of its GAD Fund.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to ensure that the GAD budget is at least five percent of the annual budget of the Municipality and to coordinate with the concerned department heads to ensure that the PPAs identified in the GPB are accordingly implemented and the corresponding budget are properly utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer, and Municipal Accountant continue to exert efforts to further reduce or eliminate the cash overdraft amount at year-end by taking appropriate measures such as exercising prudence in the setting of the target revenues and receipts during the preparation of the Annual Budget of the Municipality for the next budget period by taking into consideration the actual collections in the previous years and the Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) impact on revenue generation, and ensuring that the expenditures to be incurred will not exceed both the actual revenues and receipts and the available appropriations for the year.
observations.managementAction: The Finance Team promised to exert best efforts to reduce the cash overdraft in CY 2022. They will submit to the Municipal Mayor concrete plans and strategies to address the cash overdraft being experienced by the Municipality for several years.
observations.reason: The cash-overdraft at year-end in the General Fund had increased from P18,669,596.19 in CY 2020 to P23,943,751.32 in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor by the concerned municipal officials to refrain from using “Shopping” under Section 52.1 (a) of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and instead to resort to “Negotiated Procurement under Emergency Cases” in its succeeding procurements of COVID-19 and other disaster-related supplies and equipment or projects involving Approved Budget for the Contract/Contract Amount exceeding P100,000.00. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to ensure that their recommendations to award the contract through Shopping, Negotiated Procurement – Small Value Procurement and Negotiated Procurement – Emergency Cases in the succeeding procurements of the Municipality should be properly justified by requiring the suppliers to submit to them the following documents prescribed in Appendix A of Annex “H” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 (Updated as of March 31, 2021): 1. Mayor’s/Business Permit; 2. Omnibus Sworn Statement (For contracts with ABCs above P500,000.00); 3. Income/Business Tax Return (For contracts with ABCs above P500,000.00); 4. PCAB License (For Infrastructure Project Contracts); and 5. NFCC (For Infrastructure Project Contracts with ABCs above P500,000.00).
observations.managementAction: The audit observation was duly noted by the Bids and Awards Committee and they promised to adhere to the recommendations by (1) refraining from using “Shopping” modality in the procurement of goods that are not ordinary or regular office supplies; (2) ensuring that procurements to be undertaken thru “Negotiated Procurement under Emergency Cases” meet the necessary conditions; and (3) requiring the bidders to submit the documentary requirements prescribed in Appendix A of Annex H of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Various contracts for COVID-19 and other disaster-related supplies and equipment with contract costs more than P100,000.00 were awarded to suppliers in CY 2021 through “Shopping”. The management did not provide the reason/s for its non-implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. create an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members to
be able to complete the physical inventory in three months or less, and who
shall be temporarily relieved of all their regular duties to devote their full time
in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed, in
compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. The
Inventory Committee shall be required to perform the following:
• prepare the PIP to be submitted to the Municipal Mayor for his approval
to ensure that the conduct of inventory is properly and adequately
planned;
• submit a copy of the approved PIP to COA Audit Team at least ten (10)
calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities;
and
• conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all PPE of the
Municipality in accordance with the approved PIP and prepare the
corresponding RPCPPE complete with all the required information.
b. require the General Services Office to update the Property Cards based on
the results of the physical inventory;
c. direct the Municipal Accounting Office to update the subsidiary ledger cards
for PPE accounts; and
d. require the General Services Office and Municipal Accounting Office to
reconcile their respective records for PPE to come up with the correct
balances for PPE accounts.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the GSO to prepare the monthly RSMI duly supported with the RIS for issued
supplies and materials and submit them to the Accounting Office for proper
recording to expenses accounts.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Inventory
Committee to conduct the physical count of unissued supplies and materials at
least once a year and prepare the corresponding RPCI for submission to the COA
Auditor’s Office not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and
second semesters, respectively.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO and Municipal
Accountant to prepare and maintain SLC and SC, respectively, for each type of
supplies for the proper monitoring of the receipts, issuances, and balances of the
assets by said officials.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer,
Accountant and Treasurer to propose and submit to him a specific, measurable,
attainable, relevant and time-bound action plan that will outline their recommended
fiscal policies and strategies to address the problem of cash overdraft that the
Municipality had been experiencing over the years.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct that they refrain from using
the Special Education and Trust Funds for purposes other than those for which these
funds are intended.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to release to the Provincial and Barangay Treasurers the
provincial and barangay shares from the RPT collections of the Municipality on time
so that the concerned local government units could utilize the funds for their
projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
remit all taxes withheld within the prescribed period to avoid any liability in terms
of surcharges that may be imposed by the BIR to the latter and other concerned
officials in their personal capacity.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
immediately remit the balance of the Due to GSIS and ensure that contributions and
loan amortizations withheld are remitted on time to eliminate the risk that members-
employees may be deprived of the benefits due them.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and the
Cashier to deposit all collections intact and promptly and refrain from directly
utilizing them in the operation of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards
Committee to refrain from using “Shopping” in the procurement of goods that do
not relate to ordinary or regular office supplies and equipment and when the
Approved Budget for the Contract/Contract Cost will exceed the allowable threshold
for the Municipality and instead resort to other alternative procurement modality
such as Negotiated Procurement – Small Value Procurement or Negotiated
Procurement – Emergency Cases when circumstances permit their use, otherwise,
all the contracts should be awarded through Public Bidding.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to ensure that their
recommendation to award the contract through Shopping, Negotiated Procurement –
Small Value Procurement and Negotiated Procurement – Emergency Cases in the
succeeding procurements of the Municipality should be properly justified by
requiring the suppliers to submit the documentary requirements prescribed under
Appendix A of Annex “H” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 (Updated as of
March 31, 2021) for attachment to the disbursement voucher to prevent possible audit
suspension or disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
conduct further validation of the gross sales/receipts totaling P18,772,695.82 which
the 13 suppliers had not declared for the preceding year and enforce the collection
of the corresponding uncollected business taxes amounting to P404,419.23
including any applicable penalties and sucharges, to avoid the issuance of Notice of
Charge.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
provide the Municipal Treasurer with the List of Suppliers/Contractors who have
transacted with the Municipality in the preceding year together with the total
payments made to them as primary reference in the assessment of the business taxes
that will be collected from them.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned municipal
officials to immediately submit to the COA Auditor’s Office the distribution list or
other equivalent documents showing that the procured rice amounting to
P4,329,850.00 were duly issued or distributed to beneficiaries, for further evaluation
of the Audit Team.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to support the Journal Entry Vouchers of the issuances of relief goods
with Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) to properly document its
distributions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person to
completely fill out the GAD Accomplishment Report and disclose therein the
reasons for the deviations in the budget, as well as the factors that have hindered
the implementation of the PPAs embodied in the Municipality’s GPB.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to
ensure that the GAD budget is at least five percent of the annual budget of the
Municipality and to coordinate with the concerned department heads to ensure that
the PPAs identified in the GPB are accordingly implemented and the corresponding
budget are properly utilized.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require the Municipal Accountant and GSO to continue their efforts in reconciling and reducing the differences in their respective records of accounts for PPE with the objective of eliminating or reducing further the noted variance for PPE. b. Delegate to the GSO the acceptance of all the procured equipment and ensure the signing in the “Acceptance Portion” of the AIR and ensure the filing of Acceptance and Inspection Reports, and other relevant documents were mostly undertaken by other municipal officials instead of the General Services Officer (GSO) and deliveries were not duly supported by pictures; c. Instruct the GSO to prepare the Acknowledgement Receipt for the delivered equipment and ensure the validity of the dates of acceptance and/or inspection as indicated in the AIR and the truthfulness of the deliveries; d. Instruct the GSO to process and maintain a file of all the Purchase Requests, Purchase Orders, Acceptance and Inspection Reports, and Acknowledgement Receipt for the delivered equipment; e. Require the GSO to support the duly signed AIR with pictures of the delivered equipment which should include at least one photo of a newspaper showing its date along with the delivered equipment to ensure the validity of the dates of acceptance and/or inspection as indicated in the AIR and veracity of the deliveries; and f. Require the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher adjusting the balances of the affected accounts in the General Fund due to the recording of the cost incurred for the construction of Noveleta Medcare Center totaling P3,641,813.50 to Buildings account and the procurement of various equipment for the operations of the Noveleta Medcare Center, disaster relief and response operations, and other government operations totaling P2,274,506.45 to Expense accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to create an Inventory Committee with adequate number and complete the physical inventory of PPE. Updating of property and accounting records will likewise be undertaken by the GSO and Accounting office, respectively.
observations.reason: The discrepancy between the Accounting Records and Inventory Report had been reduced to P53,282,989.78. Reiterated in Observation No. 1, Part II of this Report The procedures for the One-Time Cleansing of the PPE accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 were not yet undertaken by the Municipality in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in all procurement of supplies and materials by recording the transactions initially as an asset using the appropriate inventory accounts prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs; b. Delegate to the GSO the acceptance of all the procured supplies and materials and the signing in the “Acceptance Portion” of the AIR; c. Create an Inspection Committee to consist of municipal officials/employees with adequate knowledge and skills to inspect the delivered goods in accordance with the technical specifications/requirements of the agency and to sign in the “Inspection Portion” of the AIR; d. Instruct the GSO to process and maintain a file of all the Purchase Requests, Purchase Orders, Acceptance and Inspection Reports for the succeeding procurements of supplies and materials; e. Require the GSO to support the duly signed AIR with pictures of the goods delivered which should include at least one photo of a newspaper showing its datealong with the delivered supplies and materials to ensure the validity of the dates of acceptance and/or inspection as indicated in the AIR and veracity of the deliveries; and f. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the procurement, and issuance or utilization of supplies and materials in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 prescribing the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO was amenable to the audit recommendations and promised to prepare the monthly RSMI duly supported with the RIS to document the issuance of supplies and to maintain stock cards.
Also, the Municipal Accountant promised to prepare and maintain the Supplies Ledger Cards for each type of inventory items.
observations.reason: The GSO was able to process and assign document number to some purchase requests, purchase orders and acceptance and inspection reports for CY 2021. Not all AIRs for CY 2021 were signed by the GSO in the “Acceptance” portion. Most of the procurement of goods in CY 2021 were not supported with pictures of the items delivered. The management did not provide the reason/s for its non-implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. refrain from making cash advances except for his traveling and confidential expenses. We also recommended that municipal officials resort to payments through check payment only when payment by check is impractical in order to minimize the unnecessary handling of cash, ensure the proper observance of procurement activities prescribed by RA No. 9184 and applicable taxes due the Bureau of Internal Revenue are withheld by the Municipality; and b. fully liquidate his outstanding cash advance balance totaling P1,349,601.00 by immediately submitting the liquidation reports and all the necessary supporting documents to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for the recording of the appropriate adjusting entries in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in all
procurement of supplies and materials by recording the transactions initially
as an asset using the appropriate inventory accounts prescribed in the
Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs;
b. Delegate to the GSO the acceptance of all the procured supplies and materials
and the signing in the “Acceptance Portion” of the AIR;
c. Create an Inspection Committee to be consisted of municipal
officials/employees with adequate knowledge and skills to inspect the
delivered goods in accordance with the technical specifications/requirements
of the agency and to sign in the “Inspection Portion” of the AIR;
d. Instruct the GSO to process and maintain a file of all the Purchase Requests,
Purchase Orders, and Acceptance and Inspection Reports for the succeeding
procurements of supplies and materials;
e. Require the GSO to prepare the Weekly SSMI duly supported by RIS for
submission to the Accounting Office for proper recording of issuances of
inventories;
f. Require the GSO to support the duly signed AIR with pictures of the delivered
supplies and materials which should include at least one photo of a newspaper
showing its date along with the delivered supplies and materials to ensure the
validity of the dates of acceptance and/or inspection as indicated in the AIR
and veracity of the deliveries; and
g. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the procurement, issuance or
utilization of supplies and materials in accordance with the COA Circular No.
2015-009 prescribing the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, being an elected official, refrain from
making cash advances except for his traveling and confidential expenses. We also
recommended that Municipal officials resort only to payments through cash
advance only when check payment is impractical in order to minimize the
unnecessary handling of cash, ensure the proper observance of procurement
activities prescribed by RA No. 9184 and applicable taxes due the Bureau of
Internal Revenue are withheld by the Municipality.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor should fully liquidate his
outstanding cash advance balance totaling P1,349,601.00 by immediately
submitting the liquidation reports and all the necessary supporting documents to
the Office of the Municipal Accountant for the recording of the appropriate
adjusting entries in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer, and
Municipal Accountant continue to exert efforts to further reduce or eliminate the
cash overdraft amount at year-end by taking or observing appropriate measures
such as exercising prudence in the setting of the target revenues and receipts
during the preparation of the Annual Budget of the Municipality for the next
budget period by taking into consideration the actual collections in the previous
years and the Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) impact on revenue
generation, and ensuring that the expenditures to be incurred will not exceed both
the actual revenues and receipts and the available appropriations for the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Development
Council, thru the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, to ensure that
all the succeeding requests for re-alignment of funds for COVID-19 response and
for other purposes are supported by a Sanggunian-approved Supplemental
Investment Program to properly guide him and the Municipal Budget Officer on
what priority programs/projects to implement and how much appropriation is
provided for each program/project, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to refrain from using
“Shopping” under Section 52.1 (a) of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and
instead resort to “Negotiated Procurement under Emergency Cases” in its
succeeding procurements of COVID-19 and other disaster-related supplies and
equipment or projects involving Approved Budget for the Contract/Contract
Amount exceeding P100,000.00.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to ensure that
their recommendations to the Head of the Procuring Entity to award the contract
to supplier for procurements to be undertaken thru “Negotiated Procurement
under Emergency Cases” are properly justified by requiring the suppliers to
submit to them the following documents prescribed in Appendix A of Annex “H”
of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 (Updated as of March 31, 2021):
1. Mayor’s/Business Permit;
2. Omnibus Sworn Statement (For contracts with ABCs above P500,000.00);
3. Income/Business Tax Return (For contracts with ABCs above P500,000.00);
4. PCAB License (For Infrastructure Project Contracts); and
5. NFCC (For Infrastructure Project Contracts with ABCs above P500,000.00).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate a task force to be consisted
of the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer, the members of the
Local Finance Committee, and Municipal Administrator, that will assist him in the
formulation of the local guidelines to properly regulate the use of the funds
appropriated for the grant of financial, medical and burial assistance to
individuals. In formulating their local guidelines, the designated task force may
take as reference the Department of Social Welfare and Development Office
Memorandum Circular No. 11 Series of 2019 for policies and procedures that may
be adopted in the municipal setting. The local guidelines should also be submitted
to the Sangguniang Bayan for their review and recommendations for improvement
and other appropriate actions.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor should approve payments for the
grant of financial, medical and burial assistance to individuals only when
supported by the following documentary requirements: 1) Duly accomplished
Social Case Study Report prepared by a qualified social worker and reviewed by
the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer, 2) Original copy of the
client’s Barangay Certificate of Residency/Indigency, 3) Photocopy of the valid
identification card of both the client/person to be interviewed and the
patient/deceased showing their signature and information on their residential
address, 4) In case of request for medical assistance, original or certified true
copy of the client’s or patient’s Medical Certificate or Medical Abstract, 5) In case
of request for burial assistance, certified true copy of the deceased person’s
Death Certificate, 6) In case of request for financial assistance for other purpose,
letter request from the client with information of his contact number, and 7) If
client is not the patient (for medical assistance) or informant (for burial
assistance), authorization letter issued to him by the patient/informant, otherwise,
all disbursements with incomplete documentation will be suspended in the audit.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget
Officer to submit to the Audit Team, for further evaluation, written explanation/
justification why the amount of P2,920,767.00 should not be disallowed in audit
considering that the amount was disbursed despite lack of appropriation, in
contravention of Section 4 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System
(GFPS) of the Municipality to maximize the utilization of the GAD Funds through
the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects as determined in its
GAD Plan and Budget for the year in order to fully attain the objectives of the
funds.

The Office of the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Office to prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for proper control and monitoring purposes. Preparation of these cards can be initially made based on the verified information contained in the RPCPPE and shall be updated regularly.

observations.managementAction: The newly appointed GSO of the Municipality assumed his office on January 03, 2020. The exerted efforts of the Municipal Accountant in the coordination with the Municipal Inventory Committee had led in the significant physical count of all the PPE in the accounting records by the Inventory Committee, hence a variance in the amount of P56,258,233.38 still existed in CY 2019.
observations.reason: The MSSMI and RIS had not been prepared for the issued supplies and materials in CY 2019 because there was no designated GSO during the year that could properly perform the said functions. The Perpetual Inventory Method had not been fully adopted in CY 2019 because the acquisition of office supplies, accountable forms, and medicines costing P1,093,588.05 were recorded directly as expenses and not as an asset using the appropriate inventory accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: require the Municipal Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to review and determine if the projects under the continuing appropriation are still feasible, if not, with the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan, re-appropriate the funds to other priority development projects to provide optimum and prompt benefits to the constituents; indicate in the 20 per cent Development Plan the timelines for the implementation of development projects and thereafter, the Bids and Awards Committee align the timing of procurement processes with the timelines set in the 20 per cent Development Plan to be able to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 5 of the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 and Section 7 of the RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to review and determine if the projects under the continuing appropriation are still feasible, if not, with the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan, re-appropriate the funds to other priority development projects to provide optimum and prompt benefits to the constituents. The utilization of the continuing appropriations for the 20 per cent Development Fund in CY 2019 amounted to P5,918,452.01 or 77.10 per cent of the available funds totaling P7,676,114.47. Moreover, the remaining balance of P1,757,662.46 had been re-appropriated for Covid-19 response programs/projects in CY 2020 pursuant to Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 2020-22 dated April 22, 2020.

observations.managementAction: The Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the MDRRMF for CY 2019 had been prepared by the Municipal Accountant and submitted to the Audit Team while audit is on-going. The unexpended balance of LDRRMF in CY 2013 of P72,336.38 had been reverted to the unexpended balance of the MDRRMF in CY 2013 to the Trust Fund on June 28, 2019 and recorded in the books of accounts under the Journal Entry Voucher No. 300-2019-06-00170.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the submission of an irrevocable standby Letter of Credit, bank guarantee, or surety bond callable upon demand, issued by a commercial bank, a surety or insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission and confirmed by the Municipality — for the advanced amount to the concerned contractor and henceforth, see to it that claims for mobilization/advance payment are supported with all the required documents thereto prior to the payment thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had availed the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) facility of its depository bank and started to implement the automated processing and payment of the salaries of its regular employees since January 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor effect the adoption of the automated payroll system to address the withdrawing and handling of cash to ensure better control over handling of cash and administrative efficiency.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality availed the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) facility of its depository bank and started to implement the automated processing and payment of the salaries of its regular employees since January 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:  a) require the concerned officials/employees to stop deducting from the payrolls the loan amortizations of the employee-borrowers from private lending institutions; b) require the employee-borrowers to transact directly with the above-mentioned private lending institutions to avoid incurrence of additional expenses and manpower resources to the government; and c) require the Treasury Office to strictly comply with Section 48 of the GAA of 2018 and Section 262 of the GAAM in the preparation payrolls.
observations.managementAction: The concerned Municipal Officials explained to the Audit Team that the deductions from the payrolls of the monthly loan amortizations could not be stopped until the loan balances of the employee-borrowers are fully settled under the existing Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipality and the private banks/lending institutions. Moreover, they informed the Audit Team that the payroll deductions for loan amortizations will be until CY 2021. The concerned Municipal Officials informed the Audit Team that the Municipality is not accepting anymore new loan applications or renewal of the existing loans to private banks/lending institutions.
observations.reason: Strict compliance to Section 48 of the GAA of 2018 and Section 262 of the GAAM could not be made because the loan amortizations from the private banks/lending institutions are still being deducted by the Municipality from the payrolls of the employee-borrowers. Several employees received a monthly net take home pay of less than R5,000.00 in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a Committee that shall thoroughly study and formulate the applicable well-defined set of guidelines, policies and procedures, containing among others, the necessary documentary requirements, criteria in the selection of the beneficiaries, amount of benefits to be provided and limitations, if necessary. After the ratification of the same by the Sangguniang Bayan, immediately submit a copy thereof to the Auditor’s Office for its guidance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The guidelines for the implementation of the Scholarship Program had been prepared and issued in CY 2019. This was also ratified in the same year by the Local Sangguniang Bayan (SB) thru SB Resolution No. 2019-45 dated June 17, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the GAD Focal Point System, to strictly adhere to the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 on planning and budgeting for PPAs that could be charged to the GAD Fund.
observations.managementAction: The amount appropriated for GAD Funds in CY 2019 of P7,873,895.46 was adequately planned and the programs, projects and activities to be charged therefrom were supported by the DILG-reviewed and endorsed GAD Plan and Budget dated September 13, 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. designate a GSO or Property Custodian who will be the over-all in charge
of the fixed assets of the Municipality, including its records such as PPE
Cards and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment;
b. create an Inventory Committee that will conduct the required annual
physical count of PPE and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE in
accordance with the afore-mentioned provisions; and
c. direct the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts to locate records
pertaining to acquisition of PPE, properly prepare the necessary
subsidiary records, maintain PPE Ledger Cards and assist the to be
designated General Services Officer or Property Custodian in
reconciliation of actual inventory vis-à-vis subsidiary records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the coordination of the
Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer in
regard to the existence of the cash deficit and come up with measures to effectively
eliminate it like conscious exercise of due diligence and prudence in the disposition
of government funds. In line thereto, we also recommend that remittance of funds
due to other government agencies be prioritized to avoid incurrence of penalties
and charges.

We recommended further that the Municipal Budget Officer see to it that
expenditures do not exceed the appropriations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• establish control measures such as centralized procurement system so
that all supplies and materials will be regulated to the actual needs of
each department/office and through the preparation of an Annual
Procurement Plan;
• consider the designation of a GSO who will handle the receipt, recording
and issuance of inventory. Thereafter, require the designated GSO to
prepare the MSSMI duly supported by RIS from the end-users for
submission to the Accounting Office for proper recording of issuances
of inventories; and
• Direct the Municipal Accountant to adopt the perpetual inventory method
in all procurement for supplies, materials and medicines.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
• the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to indicate in the 20%
Development Fund Investment Plan the timelines for the implementation of
development projects and thereafter, the Bids and Awards Committee align
the timing of procurement processes with the timelines set in the 20%
Development Plan to be able to strictly comply with the provisions of Section
5 of the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 and Section
7 of the RA No. 9184.
• the Municipal Development Council and Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator to review and determine if the projects under the
continuing appropriation are still feasible, if not, with the approval of the
Sangguniang Bayan, re-appropriate the funds to other priority development
projects to provide optimum and prompt benefits to the constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the MDRRM Council to identify
and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the MDRRMFIP the projects and
activities to be charged out of the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years to
facilitate monitoring and evaluation of the management of fund, and prepare and
submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF
on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We also recommended that the City Accountant be directed to revert to the
unappropriated surplus of the General Fund the unexpended balance of the
MDRRMF in CY 2013 to make them available for other social services or
programs/projects of the Municipality after enactment by the local sanggunian.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
enforce collection of the underpayment of business taxes, including penalties and
surcharges from the above contractor identified to have underdeclared their gross
sales. Impose penalties to the contractor for engaging business without
corresponding business permit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned Municipal officials:
1. prepare and issue policies and guidelines to address the issue on the
necessity and propriety of hiring JOs;
2. incorporate in the contract the number of man hours, quantity and duration
of the works to be performed by the job orders personnel and require them to submit daily
and quantified accomplishment reports; and
3. in coordination with the department heads, assess and review the necessity
of hiring job orders by considering the existence of regular employees who
could perform some of the contracted work. Maximize the services of the
regular employees and minimize the hiring job orders;

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to require
the submission of an irrevocable standby Letter of Credit, bank guarantee, or surety
bond callable upon demand issued by a surety or insurance company duly licensed by
the Insurance Commission and confirmed by the Municipality for the advanced amount
to the concerned contractor and henceforth, see to it that claims for
mobilization/advance payment are supported with all the required documents thereto
prior to the payment thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor effect the adoption of the automated
payroll system to address the risk of loss and/or misappropriation of funds and to
ensure better control over handling of cash and administrative efficiency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• direct the Municipal officials/employees concerned to stop deducting
from payrolls the loan amortizations of the employee-borrowers from
private lending institutions;
• require the employee-borrowers to transact directly with the above-
mentioned private lending institutions to avoid incurrence of additional
expenses and manpower resources to the government; and
• require the Treasury Office to strictly comply with Section 48 of the GAA
of 2018 and Section 262 of the GAAM in the preparation payrolls.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a Committee that shall
thoroughly study and formulate the applicable well-defined set of guidelines,
policies and procedures, containing among others, the necessary documentary
requirements, criteria in the selection of the beneficiaries, amount of benefits to be
provided and limitations, if necessary. After the ratification of the same by the
Sangguniang Bayan, immediately submit a copy thereof to the Auditor’s Office for
its guidance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the GAD Focal Point System,
to strictly adhere to the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 on
planning and budgeting for PPAs that could be charged to the GAD Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. consider designating a Property Custodian who will be the over-all in charge of the fixed assets of the local government unit, including its records such as PPE Cards and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment; 
b. create an Inventory Committee that will conduct a comprehensive inventory taking and prepare the RPCPPE, indicating therein the quantity of the property counted and grouped according to classification to facilitate reconciliation of RPCPPE with accounting records; and
c. direct the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts to locate records pertaining to acquisition of PPEs and to maintain a PPE Ledger Cards.
observations.managementAction: The Management is considering designating Property Custodian who will be the over-all in charge of the fixed assets of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Difficulty of retrieving old records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately transfer the funds Due to Special Education Fund of P866,393.54 and to Trust Fund of P21,971,686.74 to ensure that these funds are spent only for the specific purposes for which the funds were received. We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer see to it that expenditures do not exceed the appropriations. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert efforts to reduce cash deficit by exercising diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer transferred funds of P866,393.54 and P21,971,686.74. Expenditures were already within the appropriation. The concerned Municipal officials had exerted efforts to reduce cash deficit in CY 2018.
observations.reason: The Municipality had allocated funds to recoup the expenses. The fund allotted for General fund was not sufficient to cover all the expenses.

observations.managementAction: Public Bidding was already conducted. Proper evaluation was already made. All necessary supporting documents were submitted.
observations.reason: All the supporting documents had been submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Treasurer allocate funds to recoup the appropriations and funds from the General Fund to recoup the expenses charged to 20% Development Fund that were not contributory to the attainment of the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality. We further recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer stop from obligating expenditure items which are not related to and/or were not connected with the implementation of priority development programs prescribed in the abovementioned Joint Memorandum Circular against the 20% Development Fund and the Municipal Accountant to certify only to the availability of budgetary allotment to which expenditures and obligations may be properly charged.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and Treasurer refrain from obligating and charging the LDRRM Fund only expenditures which are directly related to the implementation of disaster risk management activities to conform with the guidelines set forth under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.
observations.managementAction: For the year 2018, only expenses directly related to the implementation of disaster risk management activities were charged to the fund.

observations.recommendation: We henceforth recommended that the Municipal Mayor fully establish the LDRRM Office by initiating the filling up the position of LDRRM Officer and Assistant as required under Section 12 of RA 10121 and that the LDRRMO prepare and submit the LDRRMF Investment Plan and Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO had already been established.
observations.reason: Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF still not submitted.

We also recommended that the Secretariat, Bids and Awards Committee, based on the PPMP, prepare the Annual Procurement Plan in the form prescribed in the LGU Procurement Manual, indicating therein among others, the mode of procurement and the schedule for each procurement activities.

The said set of guidelines have to be ratified by the Sangguniang Bayan.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the consultants to prepare and submit their individual accomplishment reports relevant to the periods they were paid for and henceforth, no similar claims will be processed unless these documents along with the other required relevant documents are duly attached thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Adendum to the Contracts of Consultancy services of Four consultants detailing the nature and scope of their works had been submitted. The consultants already submitted their individual accomplishment reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management refrain from hiring employees who have already reached the compulsory retirement age of 65 as Job Order personnel to ensure that the services contracted by the Municipality will be efficiently and economically carried out. We also recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer clearly define the duties and functions of the Coordinators and Members of the Mayor’s Action Team in their Job Orders, to facilitate the proper review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Not Retained JOs aged 65 and above who are still productive. The Human Resource and Management Officer to define duties and functions were still not prepared.
observations.reason: Duties and functions were still not prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor limit the hiring of Job Order (JO) personnel to what is necessary or for the exigencies of the service taking into consideration the approved budget of the Municipality. Moreover, these JO personnel must be able to document their actual duties to support the need of their services.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor limited the hiring of Job Order personnel. The number of JO employees increased by 9 from 358 in CY 2017 to 367 in CY 2018. Expenses were already within the appropriations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the prioritization of the use of the 20% Development Fund Plan in the achievement of desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the local government unit. We also recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator include only the priority development PPAs in the preparation of the Annual Investment Program specifically the appropriation for the 20% Development Fund and to monitor and evaluate its implementation in accordance with the approved development plan. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer refrain from obligating non-development expenditure items against the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: As commented by Management, they resorted in utilizing the 20% Development Fund for garbage collection and disposal in order to address the garbage problem of the Municipality. The amount allotted in the General Fund of P6.5Million was not enough to cope the cost of garbage hauling. Due to limited appropriations and funds, expenditures not related to socio- economic development were likewise charged to 20% Development Fund. However, the Budget Officer gave the assurance that proper budgeting and disbursements of funds will be observed by the Local Government Unit.
observations.reason: Due to limited appropriations and funds and the increasing demands for basic and social services, the Municipality resorted to charging administrative expenses to 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the concerned department heads of the Municipality, as end-users of the procuring entity, be required to prepare the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) to serves as a guide document in the procurement and contract implementation process. observations.reason: For the Calendar Year 2017 PPMP and APP were not yet prepared by the concerned departments; APP prepared only for office supplies and not in the prescribed form.

such as PPE Cards and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment.
b. create an Inventory Committee that will conduct a comprehensive
inventory taking and prepare the RPCPPE, indicating therein the quantity
of the property counted and grouped according to classification to facilitate
reconciliation of RPCPPE with accounting records.
c. direct the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts to locate records
pertaining to acquisition of PPEs and to maintain a PPE Ledger Cards.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately transfer the
funds Due to Special Education Fund of P866,393.54 and to Trust Fund of
P21,971,686.74 to ensure that these funds are spent only for the specific purposes
for which the funds were received.

We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer see to it that
expenditures do not exceed the appropriations.

We further recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer, Accountant and
Treasurer work hand in hand in avoiding the incurrence of cash deficit that the
municipality had been experiencing by exercising diligence, prudence and proper
control in the disposition of government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, being the head of the procuring
entity, and the Municipal Accountant who is responsible for ensuring the propriety
and completeness of supporting documents to every claim, submit separate written
justification/s as to why the services covering garbage hauling and disposal was not
procured through public bidding and payments related thereto were made despite
absence of a clear and valid contract of services, respectively.

We also recommended that henceforth, the following have to be undertaken or
strictly observed:
- that the Bids and Awards Committee ensure that procurement of services
for garbage hauling and disposal be made thru public bidding;
- that the Bids and Awards Committee ensure that the contractor is eligible
to undertake the contract for garbage hauling and disposal by strictly
adhering to the relevant provisions of Republic Act 9184;
- that the Municipal Mayor see to it that the contract of services entered into
by and between the Municipal Government clearly defined the premises
and considerations necessary in the garbage collection and disposal or
conform to the items specified in bidding documents/scope of work; and
- that the Municipal Accountant see to it that all the necessary supporting
documents to prove the validity of claims for services rendered are
attached to the disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and Treasurer allocate
appropriations and funds from the General Fund to recoup the expenses charged
to 20% Development Fund that were not contributory to the attainment of the
desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes
of the Municipality

Development Fund solely for the achievement of desirable socio-economic

unit.

programs/projects/activities that address identified gender issues with GAD

Office of the COA Auditor the aforementioned lacking required documents to avoid

necessary supporting documents to very claim be duly prepared with all the details
