observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office evaluate and address the discrepancy determining the composition of PPE accounts. The Accounting Office started determining the composition of PPE accounts. The Accounting Office started the preparation of PPE Ledger Cards.
observations.reason: The Municipality of Noveleta had no designated General Services Officer nor Property Custodian, hence the custodial functions over the property of the local government unit is still vested on the Municipal Treasurer. But due to voluminous workload in the Treasury Office, they were not able to cope with the responsibilities over the fixed assets of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant undertake the following: a. reconcile the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables with the Records of Real Property Tax Delinquencies maintained by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office; b. coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to obtain the desired information on the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables such as the name of taxpayers, amount and period of delinquencies, and other related information; c. maintain and update regularly the subsidiary ledgers.
observations.managementAction: The Real Property Tax Receivables had been reconciled with the Records of Real Property Tax Delinquencies. The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Municipal Treasurer to obtain the RPT/SET Receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the following: • no additional cash advance is granted prior to settlement of the previous advances; • as much as possible, payments should be made directly to the suppliers of goods and associations; • breakdown of the estimated expenses for projects/activities to be undertaken are attached to the disbursement vouchers; • no cash advance is granted for project to be implemented in the ensuing year; and • all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant monitored the granting and liquidation of cash advances. The Municipal Accountant required that cash advances be supported with breakdown of the estimated expenses.
observations.reason: There were cash advances granted in 2017 that were liquidated in the first quarter of 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the following measures: • the Municipal Budget Officer, Accountant and Treasurer exercise the diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of government funds. They should limit the expenditures within the total income realized during the year to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft. They should likewise find ways to cover the cash deficit with the collections in the ensuing year; • the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that all expenditures are covered with valid appropriations; • the Municipal Treasurer settle immediately the Due from General Fund in the Trust Fund books to address the cash deficit in the latter; and • the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant ensure that funds received for specific purpose is available and spent only for the purpose for which the trust was received.
observations.managementAction: Management had not able to address the cash deficit that was caused by embezzlement of the previous Municipal Treasurer (Case No. 194-05 to 215-05 archived with pending warrant raffled at Branch 16 Cavite City Trial Court). The obligations of the previous years are being paid in the current year causing the cash deficit. The Municipal Treasurer had not fully transferred the trust receipts in 2017 to General Fund. Discussed in Part II Observation No. 2 of this Report.
observations.reason: The obligations of the previous years are being paid in the current year causing the cash deficit. The Municipal Treasurer had not fully transferred the trust receipts in 2017 to General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor fully establish the LDRRM Office by initiating the filling up the position of LDRRM Officer and Assistant as required under Section 12 of RA 10121. We henceforth recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the preparation and submission of LDRRMF Investment Plan and Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF by the LDRRMO.
observations.managementAction: A meeting was called by the Local Chief Executive last May 28, 2018 for the filling up the position of LDRRMO and the Personnel Selection Board will choose who among the applicants will qualify as LDRRM Officer and Assistant. Preparation and submission of the required reports will be undertaken by the incoming Official.
observations.reason: LDRRMO post not yet filled during the year; incoming official to prepare and submit required reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate the formulation of a well defined sets of policies, procedures and guidelines pertaining to the Municipal Scholarship Program that would consider among others, the family income bracket, the intellectual capacity of the grantees and the types of school to be attended by these scholars. The said set of guidelines have to be ratified by the Sangguniang Pambayan.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to formulate a well-defined set of policies, procedures and guidelines pertaining to Municipal Scholarship Program, that will be submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan for ratification.
observations.reason: Policy formulation pending and to be submitted to Sangguniang Bayan for ratification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit immediately to the Auditor concerned the aforementioned necessary documents for each disbursement voucher. We also recommended that the concerned department heads ensure that all necessary documents to support the transactions are duly prepared with all the details and signatures required thereof. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that all disbursement vouchers are supported with documents to prove the validity and veracity of the claim.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant promised to submit immediately to the Audit Team the aforecited required documents.
observations.reason: Some required supporting documents were submitted by the Municipal Accountant; other documents were not yet prepared and attached to the disbursement vouchers prior to processing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman ensure that all bid opportunities be posted in the PhilGEPS in compliance to RA 9184 and its IRR and see to it that postings of Invitation to Bid are in "Active" status so that it can be viewed by the interested bidders to achieve transparency and competitiveness in the procurement process and be able to avail of prices most advantageous to the government.
observations.managementAction: The depository account with the PNB had been closed and its balance was transferred to LBP. (Note: Management action text for posting in PhilGEPS not fully provided.)
observations.reason: BAC could not post all bid opportunities in the PhilGEPS and could not fully access the System’s services due to errors encountered in using the system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Secretariat, Bids and Awards Committee, Municipal Treasurer and/or Municipal Engineer furnish the Audit Team with copies of approved contracts and all documents forming part thereof within five days from their execution as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require responsible officials in charge of accepting deliveries of procured items, to comply strictly with the submission of delivery documents to the Auditor within twenty-four hours upon acceptance of such deliveries pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-010.
observations.managementAction: The depository account with the PNB had been closed and its balance was transferred to LBP. (Note: Management indicated that submission of POs/contracts and delivery receipts were not fully undertaken by the Municipality.)
observations.reason: Submission of POs/contracts and delivery receipts were not fully undertaken by the Municipality due to absence of a designated General Services Officer (GSO) and decentralized procurement/delivery handling.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enforce the provision on events of default stipulated in the Contractor Agreement to facilitate the completion of the projects. We also recommended that the Municipality include in all its Purchase Orders and Contractor Agreements a provision establishing the rights to claim liquidated damages in case of breach thereof by contractor/supplier/s, in addition to the other courses of action as provided for in the IRR of RA 9184. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor rescind the contract if the total sum of the liquidated damages exceeds ten percent of the total contract price.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer monitored the implementation of the project to ensure that these were undertaken within the set timeframe.
observations.reason: Liquidated damages were imposed on two contractors for delays in the delivery of goods and completion of the projects; however, not all contracts contained LD provisions at the time of award.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer secure authority with the Sangguniang Pambayan to require taxpayers to submit Sworn Statement of all their gross sales/receipts (not only from the Municipal Government) for the preceding Calendar Year and other pertinent records/information for determination of gross receipts; submit to the Municipal Treasurer a List of Payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted with the Municipal Government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year; verify/assess the gross receipts of the validated contractors/suppliers and validate the collection; grant tax exemptions, reliefs or incentives only if supported with duly approved ordinances; properly classify and bill taxpayers their business tax in accordance with Section 2A.02 of the Municipality’s Revenue Code.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer validated the payments made by the Municipality to the suppliers and informed the Audit Team that two out of the three business establishments had closed their business, while, additional tax was collected on the other supplier.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer secure authority with the Sangguniang Pambayan to close the depository account with the Philippine National Bank and transfer its balance to Land Bank of the Philippines in compliance with Section 311 of the Local Government Code and Department of Finance Department Order No. 27-05.
observations.managementAction: The depository account with the PNB had been closed and its balance was transferred to LBP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the: 

•	  Municipal Mayor strictly utilize the 20% Development Fund in accordance with the guidelines set forth in Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) and Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011  to ensure that the purposes for which it was established is met; and

•	 expenses not specifically connected with the implementation of the development projects be charged against the regular appropriation for Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Due to limited funds and the increasing demands for basic and social services the municipality recourse to charging administrative expenses to 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Accounting Office evaluate and address the discrepancy of P24,537,684.35 between the accounting records and inventory report.
We also recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a comprehensive inventory taking and prepare the necessary report, determine and indicate in the report the quantity of the property counted and group the PPE according to classification to facilitate reconciliation of RPCPPE with accounting records.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, in the absence of a designated General Services Officer, prepare and maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the prioritization of the use of the 20% Development Plan in the achievement of desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the local government unit.
We also recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator include only the priority development PPAs in the preparation of the Annual Investment Program specifically the appropriation for the 20% Development Fund and to monitor and evaluate its implementation in accordance with the approved development plan.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer refrain from obligating non-development expenditure items against the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer comply strictly with the provision of depositing collections intact daily or not later than the next banking day with the authorized depository bank to properly safeguard government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer establish and maintain of GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formation.
We also recommended that the GAD Focal Point System include only in the GAD Plan and Budget, as well as in the Accomplishment Report, allocations that address identified gender issues, also indicate therein its objective, GAD activity and results.
Further, we recommended that the GAD Focal Person identify the gender issues/mandates, the bases of these issues and the performance indicators of the programs/projects/activities implemented and included in the GAD Accomplishment Report for 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit immediately to the Auditor concerned the aforementioned necessary documents for each disbursement voucher.
We also recommended that the concerned department  heads  ensure  that  all necessary documents to support the transactions are duly  prepared  with all the details and signatures required thereof.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it  that  all disbursement vouchers  are  supported  with  documents  to  prove  the validity and veracity of the claim.

We recommended the that the concerned department heads of the Municipality, as end-users of the procuring entity, be required to prepare the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) to serves as a guide document in the procurement and contract implementation process.
We also recommended that the Secretariat, Bids and Awards Committee, based on the PPMP, prepare the Annual Procurement Plan in the form prescribed in the LGU Procurement Manual, indicating therein among others, the mode of procurement and the schedule for each procurement activities.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman ensure that all bid opportunities be posted in the PhilGEPS in compliance to RA 9184 and its IRR and see to it that postings of Invitation to Bid are in “Active” status so that it can be viewed by the interested bidders to achieve transparency and competitiveness in the procurement process and be able to avail of prices most advantageous to the government.

We recommended that the Secretariat, Bids and Awards Committee, Municipal Treasurer and/or Engineer furnish the Audit Team with copies of approved contracts and all the documents forming part thereof within five days from their execution as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require responsible officials or in charge of accepting deliveries of procured items, to comply strictly with the submission of delivery documents to the Auditor within twenty-four hours upon acceptance of such deliveries pursuant to Commission on Audit Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 to enable the Auditor to inspect and attest that deliveries were in accordance with the specifications and made within the terms and conditions set forth in the purchase orders.

We recommended also the Municipal Mayor to enforce  the  provision  on events of default stipulated in the Contractor Agreement to facilitate the completion of the projects.
We also recommended that the Municipality include in all its Purchase Orders and Contractor Agreements a provision establishing the rights to claim liquidated damages in case of breach thereof by contractor/s and supplier/s, in addition to the other courses of action as provided for in the IRR of RA 9184.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor rescind the contract if the total sum of the liquidated damages exceeds ten percent of the total contract price.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer’s Office to require the  business  taxpayers  to submit Sworn Statement of all their gross sales/receipts (not only from  the  Municipal  Government  of  Noveleta)  for  the  preceding Calendar  Year  and  other  pertinent  records/information  for  the determination of gross receipts.
b. Municipal Accountant to submit to the Municipal Treasurer a List  of Payments made  to  contractors/suppliers  who  transacted  with  the Municipal Government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year.
c. Municipal Treasurer to  verify/assess  the  gross  receipts  of  the contractors/suppliers and validate  the  reported  under  collection totaling ₽20,587.50  and  enforce  the  collection  of  underpayment including penalties and surcharges, if applicable, pursuant  to  the rates provided  in  the  Municipality’s  Revenue  Code.  Absence  of documented action or information to counter the under collection shall mean the issuance of the corresponding Notice of Charge.
d. Municipal Treasurer to grant tax exemptions, reliefs  or  incentives only if supported with duly approved ordinances.
e. Municipal Treasurer to properly classify and bill taxpayers their business tax in accordance with Section 2A.02 of the  Municipality’s Revenue Code.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer secure an  authority  with the Sangguniang Pambayan to close  the  depository  account  with  the Philippine National Bank and transfer its balance to Land Bank of the Philippines in compliance with Section 311 of the Local Government Code and Department of Finance Department Order No. 27-05.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the OIC, General Services Office to prioritize the complete reconciliation of the balances of the PPE accounts to ascertain the reliability, existence and correctness of the Property, Plant and Equipment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Inventory Committee has been created and had started the inventory taking of PPE.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of RPCPPE with Accounting Records was only partially undertaken due to absence of complete records pertaining to PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, to be able to establish with certainty the reported RPT/SET Receivables as of year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer prepared the List of Delinquent Taxpayers and certified the Report of Total Taxable Value of Real Properties submitted by Municipal Assessor which was used as basis for the set up of Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax receivable at the beginning of the year.
observations.reason: The total amount in the Certified List of Real Property Tax Delinquencies as of December 31, 2016  was not reconciled with the year-end balance of RPT/SET Receivable.

observations.managementAction:
Management ensure that inter-agency payables and trust liabilities are utilized only for the specific purpose for which these were created or received.  



Funds collected by the Municipality during the year in behalf of other government agencies were remitted on time.
observations.reason:
Cash overdraft was a result of the embezzlement of the previous Municipal Treasurer (Case No. 194-05 to 215-05 at Branch 16 of Cavite City Trial Court).  The obligations of the previous years are being paid in the current year causing the cash deficit. 







The prior years’ inter- agency payables could not be remitted due cash deficit and these were incurred during the time of the erring Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

•	Municipal Accountant to require the municipal officials/employees concerned to submit the liquidation vouchers and its supporting documents to the Accounting Office to support the liquidation of the cash advances totaling P919,588.00; and

•	officials/employees concerned to refund the excess traveling allowance totaling P22,400.00 and henceforth follow the provisions of Executive Order No. 298 in claiming said allowance to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction:
The subject Liquidation Reports and its supporting documents were already submitted to the previous auditor.





The excess travelling allowances totaling P22,400.00 were refunded by the concerned employees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

•	direct Municipal Accountant to verify, review and analyze the  accounts Due to BIR, GSIS, PagIBIG, Philhealth  and Other NGAs to determine the existence and validity of the reported balances at year end, after which, remit to the respective agencies those that are actually for remittance; and

•	consider the request for  write-off   the balances where the existence of which were    not verified, reviewed and analyzed due to lack of documents and has been dormant in the books for  several years already.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant verified the long outstanding Inter-Agency Payables.  These payables pertain to the transactions made during the erring Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason:
The long outstanding payables could not be remitted due to the cash deficit that the Municipality had been experiencing.



The Municipality had not consider the recommendation of requesting for write-off the dormant balances for these were payable accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund of the Trust Fund book and the Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts the transfer made.
observations.managementAction: The unutilized balance of LDRRMF for 2015 was transferred on July 1, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor see to it that GAD Plan and Budget of the Municipality is submitted to the DILG office for review and approval in compliance with PCW Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality’s GAD Plan and Budget for 2016 was submitted to DILG Regional Office No. IV-A.  It was reviewed and approved by the said Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

•	   Municipal Accountant and the OIC, General Services Office comply with  the accounting and reporting guidelines on the receipt and utilization of the LDRRMF prescribed in COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 to present fairly the expense and inventory accounts of the Municipality; and

•	 require the accountable officer concerned to submit to the Audit Team the complete list of the recipients of the 345 cavans of rice and  canned goods and noodles worth P786,775.00 to avoid the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The needed documents were already submitted to the previous Audit Team.
observations.reason: Purchases of relief goods were taken as direct expenses for these were immediately distributed to the affected constituents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: 

•	Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit relevant documentary evidence that would provide clear information on the specific procurement projects that had been successfully  processed (e.g. list bidded and awarded projects and  report on the proceeds of sale of bid documents) to support the payment of honoraria totaling P120,000.00; and

•	Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to process and pay claims for honoraria only if properly supported with relevant documentary evidence  that would provide clear information on the specific procurement projects that  were  successfully bidded out to support the grant of honoraria to  the members of the BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat in compliance to DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A as amended by DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted the relevant documentary evidence to support the claim for honoraria.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the following:

•	the three disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents involving a total amount of P53,600.00 for post-audit purposes, otherwise, the covering transactions will be suspended in audit; and

•	all disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month in compliance with Section 7.2.1(a) of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts (RRSA).
observations.managementAction:
Two disbursement vouchers with aggregate amount of P51,000.00 was already submitted to the concerned Auditor, while the DV pertaining to travelling expense of Ms. Adoracion Herrera was missing and the amount involved was refunded by her.  


Disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents for CY 2016 were submitted to the Auditor’ Office.
